title: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Turnout
text: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Turnout Cavallo Hoof Boots for Turnout offer superior protection, comfort, and durability. They shield hooves from rough terrain, mud, and snow, preventing damage like thrush and white line disease. These boots provide excellent traction on slippery concrete and deep mud, reducing the risk of injury. Designed for both barefoot and shod horses, Cavallo Hoof Boots for Turnout help prevent pulled shoes and overreach injuries. They support hoof health while allowing natural movement. title: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Laminitis
h1: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Laminitis Recovery
text: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Laminitis Recovery Cavallo Hoof Boots for Laminitis provide comfort, support, and protection for horses with sore, inflamed hooves. Designed for pain relief and proven shock absorption, they help distribute weight evenly, reducing pressure on sensitive tissues. Ideal for recovery or long-term use, Cavallo Hoof Boots for Laminitis can be worn full-time with Pastern Wraps or Comfort Sleeves for added cushioning. Table of Content Understanding Laminitis Laminitis Recovery Using Cavallo Hoof Boots for Laminitis What Style of Cavallo Boot to Use for Laminitis Support Using Cavallo Hoof Boots for 24/7 Comfort Cavallo Hoof Boot Break-In Period Cavallo Testimonials Why Choose Cavallo Hoof Boots What is Laminitis? Laminitis is a painful hoof condition caused by inflammation of the laminae—the interlocking structures that support the coffin bone within the hoof. When inflamed, the laminae is weakened, compromising the coffin bone’s stability and potentially leading to rotation or sinking, which causes severe pain. For instance, imagine Velcro pulling apart—this separation within the hoof can be devastating! In short, the most common cause of laminitis is insulin-induced laminitis, often linked to insulin resistance (IR). Horses with IR struggle to regulate glucose levels because their insulin-responsive cells do not react properly to insulin. This leads to excess i

title: Cavallo Hoof Boot Break-In
text: Cavallo Hoof Boot Break-In Period The Cavallo Hoof Boot break-in period ensures your horse enjoys maximum comfort and protection. With soft, padded leather around the heel bulbs and foam cushioning that molds to the shape of your horse’s hooves, the boots provide exceptional support and reduce pressure. As you gradually break in the boots, they adapt to your horse’s unique hoof shape, offering a custom fit that prevents rubbing and discomfort. Cavallo Hoof Boots deliver lasting protection and stability, making them the perfect choice for any terrain. Table of Contents Gradual Break-In Process Water, Leather Conditioner, and Special Considerations Cavallo Custom Fit Cavallo Hoof Boot Break-In Video Cavallo Testimonials Why Choose Cavallo Hoof Boots Gradual Break-In Process: Ensuring Comfort for Your Horse When you first introduce your Cavallo Hoof Boots, it’s essential to allow your horse to break them in gradually. Like any new gear, it’s best to take a gradual approach to ensure your horse’s comfort and avoid any rubbing or soreness. For example, start by having your horse wear the boots for about 15-20 minutes per day. Further increase the time each session over several days or even weeks, depending on your horse’s sensitivity and the boot’s fit. This slow introduction allows the boots to soften and mold to the natural shape of your horse’s hooves. During the Cavallo Hoof Boo

title: Cavallo Hoof Boot Pads
h1: Cavallo Hoof Boot Pads for Added Cushioning and Support
text: Cavallo Hoof Boot Pads for Added Cushioning and Support Cavallo Hoof Boot Pads provide added cushioning and support inside your Cavallo boots, making them ideal for all horses, including those in rehabilitation. Available in six styles—Comfort, Poly, Support, Protection, Cushion, and Gel—Cavallo Hoof boot pads enhance boot fit, improve comfort, and promote healthy circulation. These pads absorb shock, reduce pressure, and protect hooves during riding, trailering, and recovery from painful conditions like laminitis, founder, and navicular. Protection Pads also prevent boot sole wear, making them perfect for shod horses. Whether for everyday use or therapeutic support, Cavallo Hoof Boot Pads help keep your horse comfortable and protected!
